data.title: Company Utilizes Stock Sales to Fund Recent Purchases
data.description:
Last week, the company made significant purchases, utilizing funds obtained from the sale of its perpetual preferred stock and common stock. According to CoinDesk, these financial maneuvers were part of a strategic effort to bolster the company's investment portfolio and enhance its market position. The decision to leverage stock sales reflects a calculated approach to capital management, aiming to optimize resources for future growth.

The perpetual preferred stock offers investors a steady dividend, while common stock provides equity ownership, both contributing to the company's financial flexibility. This strategy underscores the company's commitment to maintaining a robust financial foundation, ensuring it can seize opportunities as they arise in the dynamic market landscape. By capitalizing on these stock sales, the company demonstrates its proactive stance in navigating economic challenges and pursuing expansion initiatives.

data.title: Crypto Hacking Incidents in April Set to Reach New Record
data.description: Crypto hacking incidents in April are on track to reach a new all-time high, despite the month being less than three weeks old. According to NS3.AI, this update is based on data from DeFiLlama, as cited by Unfolded. The surge in hacking activities highlights ongoing security challenges within the cryptocurrency sector.

data.title: RAVE Experiences Dramatic Market Capitalization Fluctuations
data.description:
RAVE, a cryptocurrency, has experienced a significant fluctuation in its market capitalization, raising concerns among analysts. According to CoinDesk, the market cap of RAVE surged from $60 million to an astonishing $6 billion before plummeting back to its original value. This dramatic swing has been attributed to what some on-chain analysts describe as 'suspicious' activity linked to exchanges.

The rapid rise and fall of RAVE's market capitalization have caught the attention of the crypto community, prompting discussions about the potential causes behind such volatility. Analysts are scrutinizing the transactions and activities associated with RAVE to determine whether any manipulative practices were involved. The sudden changes in market cap highlight the inherent risks and unpredictability within the cryptocurrency market, where values can shift dramatically in a short period.

This incident underscores the importance of vigilance and due diligence for investors in the crypto space. As the market continues to evolve, understanding the factors that drive such fluctuations becomes crucial for both traders and regulators. The case of RAVE serves as a reminder of the complexities and challenges faced by the cryptocurrency industry, emphasizing the need for transparency and accountability in exchange operations.

data.title: Ripple Plans Quantum-Ready XRP Ledger by 2028 Amid Emerging Threats
data.description: Ripple has announced its intention to prepare the XRP Ledger for quantum computing by 2028, highlighting the shift of quantum threats from theoretical to credible. According to NS3.AI, the company assured that assets are currently secure but cautioned about a potential 'harvest now, decrypt later' scenario. This involves attackers storing public cryptographic data for future decryption. Ripple emphasized that the XRP Ledger's native key rotation offers better preparedness compared to other blockchains like Ethereum.

data.title: U.S. Retail Participation Rebounds Amid Bitcoin Price Concerns
data.description:
Retail participation in the United States has returned to levels seen in mid-2025, despite widespread consumer expectations of declining bitcoin prices. According to CoinDesk, this resurgence in retail activity comes as many investors remain cautious about the future trajectory of bitcoin's value. The bank's report highlights a notable shift in market dynamics, with retail investors re-engaging in the market even as they brace for potential price drops.

The renewed interest among retail participants suggests a complex sentiment in the market, where optimism about participation contrasts with apprehension about price trends. This development is significant as it indicates a potential decoupling of retail engagement from immediate price expectations. The bank's analysis underscores the resilience of retail investors, who are re-entering the market with a long-term perspective, despite short-term uncertainties surrounding bitcoin's valuation. This trend could have implications for market stability and future investment patterns.

data.title: a16z Invests in Media Company MTS
data.description: a16z has announced its investment in the media company MTS. According to Foresight News, the specific amount of the investment has not been disclosed. MTS officially launched today, positioning itself as the first 24/7 online native timeline news network. The company aims to provide real-time monitoring of developments in technology, finance, geopolitics, and culture.

data.title: AINFT Platform Integrates Trust Wallet for Enhanced Web3 Interaction
data.description: AINFT platform has announced its integration with Trust Wallet, enhancing user access to AI models on TRON and EVM networks. According to ChainCatcher, this integration allows users to log in and connect seamlessly via the Trust Wallet web plugin, while mobile users can access the EVM network through the app's built-in browser. This development aims to improve the flexibility of interactions between Web3 identities and AI services, making decentralized intelligent creation more accessible.

data.title: U.S. Justice Department Investigates Beef Companies, Media Reports
data.description: Tyson Foods shares experienced a swift downturn following reports of a criminal investigation by the U.S. Department of Justice into beef companies. According to Jin10, American media outlets have indicated that the investigation is focused on potential misconduct within the industry. The news has impacted market sentiment, leading to a decline in Tyson Foods' stock price.

data.title: Optiver Invests in AI and Crypto Venture Firm Eden Block
data.description: Optiver Holding BV has acquired an equity stake in Eden Block, a venture capital firm specializing in cryptocurrency and artificial intelligence. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing institutional interest in these technologies. This investment underscores the increasing convergence of AI and blockchain, as companies seek to leverage their potential for innovation and growth. Eden Block is known for its focus on emerging technologies, and Optiver's involvement signals confidence in the firm's strategic direction. The move reflects a broader trend of institutional backing for AI and crypto ventures, as businesses aim to capitalize on advancements in these sectors.

data.title: Apple's Price Target Raised by BNP Paribas and Bank of America Ahead of Earnings
data.description: BNP Paribas has increased its price target for Apple to $300 from $260 as of April 17, in anticipation of the company's earnings report on April 30. According to NS3.AI, Bank of America also adjusted its target, raising it to $325 from $320, while maintaining a Buy rating. Analysts are also predicting a potential buyback announcement in conjunction with the earnings release.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Alphabet and Marvell Collaborate on AI Chip Development
data.description: Alphabet and Marvell Technology are reportedly in discussions to jointly develop two artificial intelligence chips, including a new Tensor Processing Unit (TPU) designed for inference tasks. According to NS3.AI, this collaboration aims to enhance AI processing capabilities. Following the news, Marvell's shares increased by over 4.8% on Monday, while Alphabet's shares also saw a slight uptick.

data.title: Significant SENT Transfer Detected by Arkham
data.description: A large transfer of SENT tokens was detected by Arkham, involving a suspected team multisig address (0x5b54...9C0f) moving 687 million SENT to a new address (0xF9D7...262A) approximately 20 minutes ago. According to ChainCatcher, this transaction is valued at $11.52 million. Data from CMC indicates that this transfer accounts for 9.49% of the total circulating supply of SENT.

data.title: Coinbase tests AI employee agent to simulate former executives providing decision support
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that the company is testing AI agents built upon former executives to assist employees in decision-making and communication. The "Fred" agent simulates co-founder Fred Ehrsam, primarily for optimizing documents and strategy, while the "Balaji" agent simulates former CTO Balaji Srinivasan, providing innovative ideas. These AI agents will be integrated into the company's internal communication system, and in the future, employees may be able to create more agent roles themselves. Armstrong indicated that in the long run, the number of AI agents within the company may exceed the number of human employees. Furthermore, Coinbase has previously launched Agentic Wallet, which supports AI-driven autonomous transaction execution, and is advancing the x402 protocol and related payment infrastructure development, continuously exploring the application of AI and blockchain. (Decrypt)

data.title: Italy Anticipates Budget Deficit to Fall Below EU Limit Amid Growth Forecast Revision
data.description: Italy's government anticipates that the country's budget deficit will drop below the European Union's limit this year, despite a downward revision of its growth forecast due to the Iran war, Bloomberg posted on X. This expectation comes as officials adjust economic projections in response to ongoing geopolitical tensions. The revised forecast reflects the impact of the conflict on Italy's economic outlook, yet the government remains optimistic about meeting EU fiscal requirements. The situation continues to evolve as Italy navigates these challenges.

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Mixed Market Signals Amid S&P 500 and Consumer Sentiment Shifts
data.description: Bitcoin is navigating a complex market environment as the S&P 500 closed at 7,126 on April 17, while the University of Michigan's preliminary April consumer sentiment reading dropped to 47.6, marking the lowest point in the survey's history. According to NS3.AI, Bloomberg highlighted that Bitcoin's 30-day correlation with the S&P 500 increased to 0.74 in March. Additionally, SoSoValue data indicated $664 million in net inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs on April 17.

data.title: KAIO completes $8 million strategic financing round, with Tether and others participating.
data.description: KAIO, an Abu Dhabi-regulated tokenization platform, announced the completion of an $8 million strategic funding round, with participation from Tether and several crypto and institutional investors, bringing its total funding to $19 million. (CoinDesk) KAIO provides infrastructure for asset managers, supporting the tokenization and on-chain distribution of institutional funds such as BlackRock, Brevan Howard, and Hamilton Lane. The minimum investment for accredited investors is approximately $100. The company stated that it will expand into areas such as credit, structured products, and ETFs, and plans to launch an on-chain fund in partnership with Mubadala Capital, while also introducing USDT liquidity. (CoinDesk)

data.title: The Sentient team is suspected of transferring 687 million SENT tokens, worth $11.52 million, from multiple signature addresses to a new address.
data.description: According to Arkham monitoring, a suspected multi-signature address (0x5b54...9C0f) transferred 687 million SENT tokens, worth $11.52 million, to a new address (0xF9D7...262A) 20 minutes ago. According to CMC data, this transfer represents 9.49% of the total circulating supply of SENT.

data.title: U.S. CLARITY Act Faces Critical Negotiations Amid Banking Lobby Efforts
data.description:
The U.S. CLARITY Act enters a crucial negotiation phase this week, with its potential committee review in April or a delay until May hinging on recent developments. According to BlockBeats, the Senate Banking Committee will initially focus on the hearing for Federal Reserve Chair nominee Kevin Walsh. By Friday, the committee must decide whether to notify for the Act's review, aiming for a vote in the week of April 27.

Banking groups, led by the North Carolina Bankers Association, are lobbying against the Act's stablecoin yield restriction clause, urging members to contact Senator Thom Tillis's office for amendments. Industry groups are also reaching out to other committee members.

After over two months of negotiations, a compromise was reached between crypto firms and banks at the end of last month, with the crypto industry largely satisfied. However, following a White House Economic Advisory Council report minimizing the risk of stablecoin yields to the banking system, calls for amendments from the banking sector have intensified.

Patrick Witt, Executive Director of the White House Crypto Council, criticized banks on the X platform for "further lobbying out of greed or ignorance." Senator Tillis proposed a "crypto carnival" face-to-face meeting, which might extend the timeline. He emphasized that issues remain to be negotiated but expressed optimism about arranging a review in the coming weeks.

Beyond yield concerns, the Act must address ethical and DeFi-related clauses. This week's progress will determine the Act's fate, with the market closely watching developments.

data.title: Gold fell to $4,813.98, while WTI crude oil rose more than 3%.
data.description: According to the latest data from Gate, gold prices fell to $4,813.98 per ounce, a daily decrease of 0.36%. Silver prices fell to $80.005 per ounce, a daily decrease of 0.97%. The BVIX (BTC Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 43.80, a daily decrease of 1.28%. The EVIX (ETH Volatility Index) is currently quoted at 66.48, a daily increase of 0.20%. In the foreign exchange market, the USD/CNH exchange rate is unchanged, currently at 6.81558. The USD/JPY exchange rate is up 0.02%, currently at 158.652. In global stock indices, the Euro Stoxx 50 fell 0.92% to 5,987.56 points; the UK FTSE 100 fell 0.74% to 10,616.23 points; and the German DAX 40 fell 0.79% to 24,472.29 points. In commodities, WTI crude oil rose 3.57% to $90.14 per barrel, while Brent crude oil rose 3.48% to $96.14 per barrel. Gate allows users to directly trade traditional financial market products on its platform, providing a one-stop solution for precious metals, forex, global equity CFDs, major indices, and commodities, achieving deep integration of crypto assets and traditional financial assets. Gate TradFi features have been fully integrated into the Gate App and Web platform, allowing users to easily participate in global asset price trading without switching platforms, unlocking more strategies and opportunities outside the crypto market, and continuously enhancing their diversified asset allocation experience.

data.title: U.S. Senators Warn Meta and Alphabet Over Russian Disinformation
data.description: U.S. Senators have issued warnings to Meta Platforms and Alphabet regarding the spread of Russian disinformation. According to Jin10, the senators are concerned about the impact of misleading information originating from Russia on social media platforms operated by these companies. The warnings highlight the ongoing challenges in managing content and ensuring the integrity of information shared online. The senators urge Meta and Alphabet to take more stringent measures to prevent the dissemination of false narratives that could influence public opinion and undermine democratic processes.
